text.title: workflow-design-phase Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for workflow-design-phase as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Architecture decisions made without running existing code can contradict hidden constraints.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require agents to test assumptions against the actual codebase during the research phase.

failure-mode.description: Phase 9 (user review) is a synchronous human checkpoint — async agent pipelines stall here.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Build queuing for human review gates; do not block agent pipeline.

failure-mode.description: Codebase research via Grep/Glob misses runtime behavior (dynamic imports, factory patterns).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Ask research agents to also check git log for recent changes and test files for runtime patterns.

failure-mode.description: Wave analysis speedup estimates are theoretical; I/O-bound tasks rarely parallelize as predicted.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Apply a 0.5x multiplier to theoretical speedup when reporting timelines.

failure-mode.description: The reviewer agent checks structure but cannot validate that AD choices are technically correct.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Include a domain expert (or a second Opus agent) in Phase 10 review for non-trivial architectures.
